summary: Phase 1 – CSS Token Refactor: Replaced all theme-variant color tokens in `src/styles/global.css` with `color-mix(in oklch, ...)` expressions driven by a new `--theme-mix` CSS custom property (0 = dark, 1 = light). Shadow variables (box-shadow shorthand) cannot be interpolated via color-mix, so they retain light-mode overrides in `[data-theme='light']`. Section background aliases also kept in `[data-theme='light']` because they swap values (not just change them). The no-JS OS-preference media query was updated to set `--theme-mix: 1` for light-pref users.

Phase 2 – FOWT Prevention: Updated the inline `<script is:inline>` in `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` to restore `--theme-mix` from `localStorage('theme-mix')` and apply it as an inline style on `<html>` before CSS renders. Includes backward-compat fallback for old `theme` key ('dark'→0, 'light'→1) and OS-preference default.

Phase 3 – ThemeSlider Component: Created `src/components/ThemeSlider.astro` with an `<input type='range'>` (0–100), moon icon (left, dark), sun icon (right, light), full cross-browser CSS (WebKit + Firefox thumb/track), a module script that syncs both slider instances and persists to `localStorage('theme-mix')`, and an OS-preference media query listener.

Phase 4 – Integration: Updated `src/components/Nav.astro` to replace the import and both usages of `<ThemeToggle />` with `<ThemeSlider />` — one in `.nav-right` (desktop) and one in `.nav-overlay` (mobile). The existing `theme-changed` CustomEvent dispatch from ThemeSlider closes the mobile overlay on slider interaction.
